chunks.text: U.C. Berkeley has the top ten most AMiner Most Influential Scholar Award winners across all fields of computer science in 2016 and the top five most award winners in the fields of Computer Vision, Database, Machine Learning, Multimedia, Security, Computer Networking, and System. The influence that our Computer Science faculty have had over their various fields last year is impressive in both magnitude and scope. The 28 CS faculty members included in the rankings were among the 100 most-cited authors in 12 of the 15 research areas evaluated.

Out of the 700,000 researchers indexed, only 16 appeared on three or more area top 100 lists. Two of our faculty were among the 100 most-cited authors in 3 different areas: Scott Shenker (#1 Computer Networking, #51 in System, #99 in Theory) and Trevor Darrell (#8 in Multimedia, #18 in Computer Vision, #100 in Machine Learning).

Of the 113 scholars who appeared in 2 areas lists, 10 were Berkeley CS faculty: Jitendra Malik, Ion Stoica, Michael Franklin, Joseph Hellerstein, Richard Karp, Vern Paxson, Marti Hearst, Stuart Russell, Randy Katz, and Dave Patterson. An additional 15 faculty ranked in single area lists.

3 CS faculty (Dawn Song, Jitendra Malik, and Scott Shenker) were the most cited authors in their fields and 8 others were among the top 10. Rankings by Area: Computer Networking (6 faculty, top rank Scott Shenker #1), Computer Vision (3 faculty, top rank Jitendra Malik #1), Security (4 faculty, top rank Dawn Song #1), Database (5 faculty, top rank Michael Franklin #10).

Machine Learning (4 faculty, top rank Michael Jordan #2), System (8 faculty, top rank Ion Stoica #24), Natural Language Processing (2 faculty, top rank Daniel Klein #8), Multimedia (1 faculty, Trevor Darrell #8, plus graduate students ranked 12 and 13), Theory (4 faculty, top rank Christos Papadimitriou #10), Human-Computer Interaction (1 faculty, Marti Hearst #17), Artificial Intelligence (1 faculty, Stuart Russell #18), Computer Graphics (3 faculty, top rank Jitendra Malik #23).

The AMiner Most Influential Scholar Annual List names the world’s top-cited research scholars from the fields of science and engineering. The list is conferred in recognition of outstanding technical achievements with lasting contribution and impact to the research community. The 2016 winners are among the most-cited scholars from the top venues of their respective subject fields as of 2016. Recipients are automatically determined by a computer algorithm deployed in the AMiner system that tracks and ranks scholars based on citation counts collected by top-venue publications.
